curation_notes: The vulnerability is in get_misp_connection() in util.py which uses a module-level global variable misp_connection as a singleton cache. In remote-transform mode, multiple users share the same process, so the first user's authenticated connection is returned to all subsequent users. The patch replaces the global function with a MISPConnection class that creates a new connection per invocation. Four source files were changed (util.py plus three transform callers: attributetoevent.py, eventtoattributes.py, galaxytoevent.py), making this a noisy patch. The primary vulnerability location is the get_misp_connection function in util.py; the other files are callers updated to use the new class. The pre-assigned class auth-bypass (from CWE-284) is correct — the vulnerability allows one user's authenticated session to be used by other users.
